metadata.title: Product Experiences
metadata.description: Outcome-based product experiences for the eMobility domain, grounded in open charging interoperability, network operations, fleet workflows, and settlement needs.

definitions.productBrick: A reusable implementation-facing building block used to realize one or more product experiences.
definitions.productExperience: A high-level, outcome-based experience that delivers customer or business value.

metadata.flowModelNote: Each experience now carries only experience-specific flows. Each flow step lists the bricks and external systems used inside that experience.

experiences.name: Connect Any Charger
experiences.description: Help a charge point operator bring new hardware online quickly regardless of vendor.

flows.title: Connect Any Charger
flows.description: Help a charge point operator bring new hardware online quickly regardless of vendor.

steps.title: Register charger
keyFacts: This step determines how quickly users reach initial value.
It often combines setup effort, policy choices, and correctness checks.
painPoints: Users stall when setup asks for too much context too early.
Errors here create rework and reduce confidence in the product model.

dependencies.name: Charge Point Connectivity & Protocol Gateway
dependencies.description: Securely connects to chargers via OCPP/OCPP-compliant protocols and manages real-time device interactions.
dependencies.how: Uses Charge Point Connectivity & Protocol Gateway for chargers via OCPP/OCPP-compliant protocols and manages real-time device interactions in register charger.

dependencies.name: EV Chargers
dependencies.description: Provide the physical charging endpoints.
dependencies.how: Relies on EV Chargers for the physical charging endpoints in register charger.

steps.title: Map charger protocol
keyFacts: Map charger protocol is a meaningful step in the charge point operator experience flow.
Users need clear guidance and feedback here to keep moving forward.
painPoints: Users drop out when map charger protocol feels fragmented across channels.
Confidence decreases when the product does not explain what happens next.
